models.candidate: Candidate context for the briefing header.
models.jobRequisition: Job requisition context for the briefing header.
models.briefing: The AI-generated briefing metadata including interview type (HR or Technical), match assessment, and generation status.
models.strength: A candidate qualification that meets or exceeds a job requirement.
models.gap: A missing or unclear qualification that should be explored in the interview.
models.riskArea: A discrepancy between data sources that warrants probing.
models.interviewQuestion: An AI-generated question linked to a gap or risk area, tailored to the interview type.

relationships: Briefing is generated from CandidateProfile and JobRequisition with interview type context
Strengths, Gaps, and RiskAreas are extracted from the profile-to-requisition comparison
InterviewQuestions are linked to specific Gaps or RiskAreas
Questions are oriented to either HR screening or technical evaluation

briefing.matchSummary: Maria Chen is a strong match for the Senior Backend Engineer role. Her 8 years of experience in distributed systems at fintech companies (Plaid, Stripe) aligns well with the position's requirements. Key strengths in Python, Go, and event-driven architectures. A few areas merit deeper technical exploration in the interview.

strengths.title: Distributed Systems Expertise
strengths.detail: Led migration to event-sourced architecture at Plaid. Deep experience with Kafka, gRPC, and high-throughput pipelines.
strengths.requirementMatch: Core requirement: Design and maintain distributed backend services

strengths.title: High-Throughput System Design
strengths.detail: Built fraud detection system processing 50K events/sec at Stripe. Reduced P99 latency by 75% at Plaid.
strengths.requirementMatch: Core requirement: Performance optimization and scalability

strengths.title: Python + Go Proficiency
strengths.detail: Production experience in both Python and Go across multiple companies, with strong LinkedIn endorsements (47 Python, 23 Go).
strengths.requirementMatch: Core requirement: Python and Go fluency

strengths.title: Fintech Domain Knowledge
strengths.detail: 3+ years at Plaid and 2.5+ years at Stripe working on payment processing and financial data infrastructure.
strengths.requirementMatch: Preferred: Financial services or regulated industry experience

strengths.title: Technical Mentorship
strengths.detail: Mentored 4 junior engineers through structured growth plans at Plaid.

gaps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Ownership
gaps.detail: Resume and LinkedIn don't mention CI/CD or deployment pipeline work. Unclear if she has led or significantly contributed to CI/CD improvements.

gaps.title: System Design Communication
gaps.detail: Technical accomplishments are well-documented, but no evidence of presenting architecture decisions to non-technical stakeholders.
gaps.sourceNote: No conference talks, blog posts, or cross-functional communication evidence

gaps.title: Observability Tooling
gaps.detail: Latency reduction work implies observability usage, but no specific mention of monitoring stacks (Datadog, Grafana, etc.).
gaps.sourceNote: Implied but not stated in any source

riskAreas.title: Title Discrepancy
riskAreas.detail: Resume says 'Senior Software Engineer' while LinkedIn says 'Senior Backend Engineer'. Minor, but worth clarifying.

riskAreas.title: Terraform Experience Depth
riskAreas.detail: Terraform listed on resume but absent from LinkedIn skills and ATS tags. May indicate surface-level familiarity.

interviewQuestions.question: Walk me through the event-sourced architecture migration at Plaid. What were the key design decisions, what trade-offs did you evaluate, and how did you handle backward compatibility?
interviewQuestions.context: Probes depth of distributed systems expertise and decision-making in the architecture migration mentioned in resume highlights.

interviewQuestions.question: You reduced P99 latency from 480ms to 120ms. Can you break down the bottlenecks you identified and the specific optimizations you applied?
interviewQuestions.context: Tests whether the performance work was hands-on engineering versus delegation, and reveals observability tooling experience.

interviewQuestions.question: Tell me about your experience with CI/CD pipelines. Have you owned or significantly improved deployment infrastructure at any of your roles?
interviewQuestions.context: Directly addresses the CI/CD gap — not mentioned in any source material.

interviewQuestions.question: How do you approach monitoring and observability in production systems? What tools and practices have you used?
interviewQuestions.context: Addresses the observability gap — latency work implies usage but no specific tooling mentioned.

interviewQuestions.question: What is your hands-on experience with Terraform? Can you describe a specific infrastructure-as-code project you led or contributed to?
interviewQuestions.context: Clarifies depth of Terraform experience — listed on resume but absent from LinkedIn and ATS.

interviewQuestions.question: Tell me about mentoring the 4 junior engineers at Plaid. How did you structure their growth plans and measure progress?
interviewQuestions.context: Explores mentorship approach and leadership readiness for a senior role.

interviewQuestions.question: You've spent your career in fintech. What draws you to this role specifically, and how do you see your career evolving from here?
